Case title: Citation-hallucination checks in AI-assisted literature-review drafts
Short description: Track when humans verify or fail to verify citations inserted by AI during literature-review drafting and evidence synthesis.
Empirical unit of analysis: Draft review sections, bibliographies, and verification logs
Candidate roles tested: Accountability and labor allocator; protocol and provenance architect
Key typology question: Is provenance architecture distinct from accountability, or are they the same role in literature review practice?
Observable artifacts / traces: Draft histories, bibliography correction logs, fake-reference incidents, disclosure notes
Key outcomes observed: Citation error rates, verification burden, correction timing, responsibility assignment
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Citation support tools eliminate hallucination risk without extra human verification work
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Poor citation habits could predate AI and inflate apparent AI effects
Why this case has high leverage: This case can show whether two typology categories collapse in practice.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
